GAP: a space or opening between two things
GATHER: to bring together in one place
GENERATE: to produce or bring into existence
GENERATION: all people born around same time
GENESIS: the origin or beginning of something
GENUINE: truly what it claims to be
GIANT: person or thing of great size
GIVE: to freely transfer possession of something
GLANCE: to take brief or hurried look
GLARE: to look angrily; harsh bright light
GLEAM: to shine brightly; flash of light
GLIMMER: to shine faintly with wavering light
GLITTER: to shine with bright flashing light
GOAL: object of person's ambition or effort
GOVERN: to control and direct affairs of
GOVERNMENT: system by which state is governed
GRACEFUL: moving in smooth and attractive way
GRANT: to agree to give or allow
GRAVE: serious or solemn; place of burial
GRAZE: to feed on grass; touch lightly
GREAT: above average in size or ability
GREET: to give polite word of recognition
GROAN: to make deep sound expressing pain
GROUP: number of people or things together
GROW: to increase in size or develop
GUARANTEE: formal promise that conditions will be met
GUARDIAN: person responsible for care of another
GUEST: person invited to visit or stay
GUIDE: to lead or show the way
GUIDELINE: general rule or piece of advice
GUSH: to flow forth suddenly and violently
